things.name: Vieux-Port
things.description: Ancient harbour at the heart of city life with daily fish market and ferries to islands

things.name: MuCEM
things.description: Museum of European and Mediterranean Civilisations in a striking contemporary building

things.name: Notre-Dame de la Garde
things.description: Marseille's beloved hilltop basilica visible from across the city and sea

things.name: Calanques National Park
things.description: Dramatic limestone inlets with turquoise water perfect for hiking, swimming and kayaking

things.name: Chateau d'If
things.description: Island fortress immortalised by Dumas in The Count of Monte Cristo, reachable by ferry

things.name: Le Panier
things.description: Oldest district in the city with colourful streets, artisan shops and street art

things.name: Palais Longchamp
things.description: Monumental 19th-century palace housing the Fine Arts and Natural History museums

things.name: Frioul Islands
things.description: Archipelago of four islands with beaches, hiking and rare flora accessible by ferry

things.name: Abbaye Saint-Victor
things.description: One of France's oldest churches with 4th-century catacombs and remarkable carved sarcophagi

things.name: Cours Julien
things.description: Vibrant neighbourhood with street art, independent music venues and eclectic restaurants
